forecast-period.text: A high pressure system southwest of Perth extends a ridge over SA. The ridge will weaken overnight to allow the tail end of a cold front to brush the southeast of SA later Thursday. The high is forecast to move east on Friday to be southeast of Tasmania by Sunday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.text: Variable below 10 knots.
Below 0.5 metres.
Westerly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Mostly clear.
West to northwesterly about 10 knots becoming south to southwesterly in the late afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
Westerly 2.5 to 3 metres, tending southwesterly 2.5 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy.
Southerly 10 to 15 knots tending southeasterly 15 to 25 knots before dawn.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning, then increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres by early evening.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy.
East to southeasterly 15 to 20 knots.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 2 metres during the evening.
Mostly sunny.
